current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.
recent_insights: round 1: agent stopped: Stopped without code changes because the local execution layer is misconfigured. I attempted to read `research/program.md` and list `sparsify/`, but every command failed before startup with the same bubblewrap error, so I could not inspect the required guidance or modify code responsibly.
next_hypotheses: Establish or refresh a trustworthy K=128 quality anchor before rewarding lower-K points.
Do not treat smaller K as success unless its FVU is materially better than the current K=128 baseline.
Prefer experiments that can plausibly cut the current K=128 FVU by about half, even if they keep K unchanged at first.
